verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 encodes HSP60 (mitochondrial chaperonin), whose canonical location is the mitochondrial matrix. However, multiple studies document a bona fide plasma membrane form of HSP60 on the surface of various cell types—particularly tumor cells, stressed cells, and cells undergoing ICD. This surface HSP60 has been validated by flow cytometry on intact (non-permeabilized) cells and by antibody binding to living cells, and it persists through wash steps. Because surface expression is induced by stress/oncogenic transformation rather than constitutive, this is best classified as contextual/cell_state_induced.
